title: azalea oleifolia
intro: Azalea oleifolia, also known as the tea azalea or mountain azalea, is a flowering plant species native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its white or pink flowers and shiny leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and flowers, which have been employed to treat various health issues due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Azalea oleifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate sore throats, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Azalea oleifolia is often prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
